steps.title: I'm Going To... 📅
steps.desc: Learn to talk about future plans! Dutch uses 'gaan + infinitive' just like English 'going to'.

steps.note: Future = gaan (conjugated) + infinitive. 'Ik ga werken' = I'm going to work. Super simple!

steps.example: Morgen ga ik naar de dokter.
steps.exampleEn: Tomorrow I'm going to the doctor.
steps.note: Time word for future. 'Morgen' at start OR end of sentence. Both work!
'Volgende' = next/following. Also: volgende maand (next month), volgend jaar (next year).

steps.en: to plan to / to intend to
steps.example: Ik ben van plan om te verhuizen.
steps.note: More formal than 'gaan'. 'Ik ben van plan om...' + te + infinitive. Shows intention.
Adds uncertainty. 'Misschien' usually at start of sentence. Very common in Dutch!

steps.example: Ik ga zeker naar het feest.
steps.exampleEn: I'm definitely going to the party.
steps.note: Shows certainty. 'Zeker weten' = know for sure. Opposite of 'misschien'.

steps.en: soon / later today / in a bit
steps.exampleEn: I'm going shopping in a bit.
steps.note: 'Straks' = soon/later today. Very useful! 'Tot straks!' = See you later (today)!
'Binnenkort' = soon (within weeks/months). More distant than 'straks'.
'Later' = later. Can mean later today, or later in general. Context tells you which!

steps.exampleEn: What are you going to do tonight?
steps.note: 'Vanavond' = tonight/this evening. Also: vanmiddag (this afternoon), vannacht (tonight/this night).

steps.title: 🗣️ Dialogue: Weekend Plans
steps.text:
A: Hé, wat ga je dit weekend doen?
B: Ik ga misschien naar het strand. En jij?
A: Ik ben van plan om te fietsen.
B: Leuk! Ga je zaterdag of zondag?
A: Waarschijnlijk zaterdag. Zondag ga ik familie bezoeken.
B: Klinkt goed! Veel plezier!
A: Jij ook! Tot volgende week.

Translation:
A: Hey, what are you going to do this weekend?
B: I might go to the beach. And you?
A: I'm planning to cycle.
B: Nice! Are you going Saturday or Sunday?
A: Probably Saturday. Sunday I'm visiting family.
B: Sounds good! Have fun!
A: You too! See you next week.

steps.title: 🔮 Future with GAAN
steps.text:
Dutch future is super easy:

📘 Pattern: gaan (conjugated) + infinitive

Ik ga werken — I'm going to work
Jij gaat studeren — You're going to study
We gaan eten — We're going to eat
Zij gaan reizen — They're going to travel

⏰ Time expressions:
morgen — tomorrow
straks — soon/later today
volgende week — next week
volgend jaar — next year
binnenkort — soon
later — later
vanavond — tonight

💡 Word order:
Morgen ga ik werken. (time first)
Ik ga morgen werken. (time after verb)
Both are correct!

steps.q: How do you say 'I'm going to eat'?

steps.hint: Next week I AM free (not going)

steps.title: Let's Meet! 🤝
steps.desc: Make plans and schedule appointments in Dutch. Essential for social life and work!

steps.note: THE way to suggest plans! 'Zullen we + infinitive?' Very polite and common.

steps.en: to make an appointment / to arrange to meet
steps.note: 'Een afspraak' = an appointment. 'Afspreken' = to arrange to meet. Super useful verb!

steps.en: That works out well / That's convenient
steps.example: Dinsdag? Ja, dat komt goed uit!
steps.note: Perfect response when a time suits you. 'Uitkomen' = to work out/suit.

steps.en: That doesn't work / That's not convenient
steps.note: Polite way to say a time doesn't suit. Much better than just 'nee'!
Essential question for making plans. 'Laat' = late, but 'hoe laat' = what time.
'Om' = at (for time). 'Om acht uur' = at 8 o'clock. Drop 'uur' in casual speech: 'om drie'.
Perfect way to confirm plans! Past participle of 'afspreken' used as exclamation.
'Tot' = until/see you. 'Tot dan' = see you then. Also: Tot morgen! (See you tomorrow!)

steps.example: Ik kan niet. Een ander keertje?
steps.note: Polite way to decline but stay friendly. 'Keertje' = diminutive of 'keer' (time/occasion).

steps.en: Does it work? / Can you make it?
steps.exampleEn: Tomorrow at 10, can you make it?
steps.note: 'Lukken' = to succeed/work out. 'Lukt het?' = casual way to ask if someone can make it.

steps.title: 🗣️ Dialogue: Making an Appointment
steps.text:
A: Hé Lisa, zullen we binnenkort koffie drinken?
B: Ja, leuk! Wanneer heb je tijd?
A: Donderdag of vrijdag?
B: Donderdag komt niet uit, maar vrijdag is prima.
A: Oké! Hoe laat?
B: Om 3 uur?
A: Dat komt goed uit! Bij het café op de hoek?
B: Perfect. Afgesproken!
A: Top! Tot vrijdag dan.
B: Tot dan!

Translation:
A: Hey Lisa, shall we have coffee soon?
B: Yes, nice! When do you have time?
A: Thursday or Friday?
B: Thursday doesn't work, but Friday is fine.
A: Okay! What time?
B: At 3 o'clock?
A: That works! At the café on the corner?
B: Perfect. It's a deal!
A: Great! See you Friday then.
B: See you then!

steps.title: 🤝 Making Plans Like a Dutch Person
steps.text:
Standard flow:

1️⃣ SUGGEST:
Zullen we koffie drinken? — Shall we have coffee?
Heb je zin om te lunchen? — Do you feel like lunch?

2️⃣ PROPOSE TIME:
Morgen om 3 uur? — Tomorrow at 3?
Volgende week dinsdag? — Next week Tuesday?

3️⃣ ACCEPT:
Ja, leuk! — Yes, nice!
Dat komt goed uit! — That works!
Goed plan! — Good plan!
Prima! — Fine!
Lukt het? — Can you make it?

4️⃣ DECLINE:
Dat komt helaas niet uit. — That unfortunately doesn't work.
Ik kan niet, sorry. — I can't, sorry.
Een ander keertje? — Another time?

5️⃣ CONFIRM:
Afgesproken! — Agreed!/It's a deal!
Tot dan! — See you then!
Tot morgen! — See you tomorrow!

steps.title: My Plans 🎯
steps.desc: Talk about your future goals, dreams, and intentions. From weekend plans to life goals!

steps.en: I would like to... / I want to...
steps.exampleEn: I would like to learn Dutch.
steps.note: 'Willen' (to want) + infinitive. Add 'graag' to be polite. 'Ik wil' alone = direct/demanding.

steps.example: Ik ben van plan om te verhuizen.
steps.note: Formal intention. 'Van plan zijn om + te + infinitive'. Shows serious planning.

steps.exampleEn: Hopefully it will be nice weather.
steps.note: Expresses hope. Usually at start of sentence. From 'hopen' (to hope).
'Hopen dat' + sentence. The 'dat' is important! 'Ik hoop dat het lukt' = I hope it works out.

steps.example: Als alles goed gaat, verhuis ik in mei.
steps.exampleEn: If all goes well, I'll move in May.
steps.note: Common phrase for conditional future. Shows cautious optimism — very Dutch!

steps.en: in a month / a month from now
steps.example: Over twee weken ga ik op vakantie.
steps.exampleEn: In two weeks I'm going on vacation.
steps.note: 'Over' = in (future time). Over een jaar = in a year. Over drie dagen = in three days.
Shows probability. Middle ground between 'misschien' and 'zeker'.
Noun form. 'Wat zijn je plannen?' = What are your plans?

steps.example: In de toekomst wil ik reizen.
steps.exampleEn: In the future I want to travel.
steps.note: 'Toekomst' = future (noun). Related to 'toekomen' (to arrive/come to).

steps.title: 🗣️ Dialogue: Life Plans
steps.text:
A: Wat zijn je plannen voor volgend jaar?
B: Ik ben van plan om een nieuwe baan te zoeken.
A: Oh ja? Wat wil je graag doen?
B: Hopelijk iets met ICT. En jij?
A: Ik wil graag naar het buitenland verhuizen.
B: Wauw! Waarheen?
A: Waarschijnlijk naar Spanje of Portugal.
B: Als alles goed gaat, lukt het zeker!
A: Dank je! Ik hoop het.

Translation:
A: What are your plans for next year?
B: I'm planning to look for a new job.
A: Oh yeah? What would you like to do?
B: Hopefully something with IT. And you?
A: I'd like to move abroad.
B: Wow! Where to?
A: Probably to Spain or Portugal.
B: If all goes well, it'll definitely work out!
A: Thanks! I hope so.

steps.title: 🎯 Talking About Future Plans
steps.text:
Different levels of commitment:

💪 CERTAIN:
Ik ga morgen... — I'm going to tomorrow...
Volgende week doe ik... — Next week I'll do...
Ik ga zeker... — I'm definitely going to...

📝 PLANNED:
Ik ben van plan om... — I'm planning to...
Ik wil graag... — I'd like to...

🤞 HOPEFUL:
Hopelijk... — Hopefully...
Ik hoop dat... — I hope that...
Als alles goed gaat... — If all goes well...

❓ UNCERTAIN:
Misschien... — Maybe...
Ik denk dat... — I think that...
Waarschijnlijk... — Probably...

Example progression:
Misschien ga ik volgende jaar verhuizen.
→ Ik ben van plan om te verhuizen.
→ Als alles goed gaat, verhuis ik in mei.
→ Ik ga zeker verhuizen!

steps.q: Most polite way to express a wish?

steps.title: 🎯 Future Expressions Summary
steps.text:
FUTURE IN DUTCH:

1️⃣ GAAN + infinitive (most common)
Ik ga werken — I'm going to work
We gaan eten — We're going to eat

2️⃣ PRESENT TENSE (with time word)
Morgen werk ik — Tomorrow I work
Volgende week zijn we vrij — Next week we're free

3️⃣ ZULLEN (formal/suggestions)
Zullen we gaan? — Shall we go?
Dat zal wel lukken — That will probably work

4️⃣ WILLEN (desires/intentions)
Ik wil studeren — I want to study

5️⃣ VAN PLAN ZIJN (firm plans)
Ik ben van plan om te verhuizen — I plan to move

No separate future tense like English 'will'!
Dutch uses present + time word or 'gaan'.
